free_text: Excitation functions for the production of 82Sr by
proton bombardment of natRb at energies up to 100 MeV
M.Sc.Thesis submitted to Univ. of the Western Cape
Each stack consisted of thin RBCL-target samples,
The target samples-RBCL-tablets were prepared by
pressing the salt of highest purity-more than 99.9%.
The salt was dried under vacuum conditions and small
quantities then were weighed and pressed into thin
disc shaped samples, diameter 19-mm each, with
Aluminium and copper foils, diameter of 19-mm each, of
highest purity more than 99.9% were obtained.
Total system dead time was maintained below 5%.
Irradiated by nominal 0.1 uA beam for 1 hour
Compared with IAEA recommended cross section
Decay losses during and after irradiation
Estimated to be accurate within +/-1 MeV,
Quadrature sum of all the contributing
On. Data in Buthelezi's thesis adopted
